# Web Shared Foundation — Zone Context

  packages/web/src/shared/index.ts → packages/web/src/shared/node-culler.ts {NodeCuller}

Incoming (other zones → this zone):

</imports>

<findings>

[observation] [info] Coupling of 0.56 is moderate but expected for a shared-foundation zone with multiple upstream consumers; the metric reflects correct usage, not structural debt.
[observation] [info] The 3-file zone cleanly separates data-file constants (data-files.ts) from tree-pruning logic (node-culler.ts), maintaining single-responsibility within the foundation layer.
[observation] [info] web-shared is the only web-package zone with zero framework dependencies; any PR adding a Preact or Express import here should be rejected as an architectural violation.
[pattern] [info] web-shared-foundation and mcp-route-layer have no import relationship (direct or transitive through shared types), confirming correct layering: the composition-root tier does not bypass the application tier to access foundation utilities.
[relationship] [warning] web-shared-foundation is a pure sink in the web package import graph — all 7 cross-zone imports are inbound with zero outbound, making it the only zone whose public API changes carry monorepo-wide ripple risk proportional to the 387-file consumer zone above it.
[anti-pattern] [warning] node-culler.ts is a viewer-exclusive IntersectionObserver engine (its docblock explicitly states 'Preact integration is handled in the CulledNode component') but lives in web-shared-foundation, a zone intended to be framework-agnostic and shared between server and viewer. Its presence in the foundation zone inflates web-shared-foundation's blast radius to 387 files for a module that is only ever consumed by the viewer layer. Moving it to src/viewer/ would narrow the foundation to data-files.ts (the only truly shared constant) and reduce unnecessary coupling surface.
[suggestion] [warning] Add a build-time or test-time validation that the key sets exported from DATA_FILES and SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES in data-files.ts are consistent between the server build artifact and the viewer build artifact. Currently a key rename would silently break HTTP fetches at runtime — this dual-artifact consumption pattern has no compile-time safety net.
[suggestion] [info] After moving node-culler.ts to src/viewer/, evaluate dissolving web-shared-foundation as a zone: the remaining 2 files (data-files.ts + index.ts barrel) are functionally a single constants module. Inlining data-files.ts into a web-level constants location would eliminate the zone, reduce zone graph complexity, and make the constants' blast radius explicit at the point of definition rather than hidden behind a zone boundary.

</findings>

<insights>

- With only 3 files, the zone is appropriately minimal for a foundation layer; resist the temptation to add framework-specific helpers here, as doing so would violate the web-shared architectural contract.
- Coupling of 0.56 is higher than expected for a leaf-level foundation — the 7 inbound imports from web-viewer confirm it acts as a shared foundation rather than a standalone module, which explains the elevated coupling metric.
- The index.ts barrel makes the public surface explicit; keeping all exports funneled through it ensures downstream consumers don't bypass the intended API surface.
- Coupling of 0.56 is moderate but expected for a shared-foundation zone with multiple upstream consumers; the metric reflects correct usage, not structural debt.
- The 3-file zone cleanly separates data-file constants (data-files.ts) from tree-pruning logic (node-culler.ts), maintaining single-responsibility within the foundation layer.
- web-shared is the only web-package zone with zero framework dependencies; any PR adding a Preact or Express import here should be rejected as an architectural violation.
- The zone has no outbound zone-crossing imports (all 0.56 coupling is inbound fan-in from web-viewer-application) — this is the only zone in the web package where coupling is entirely inbound, confirming its role as a true leaf-level foundation with no external dependencies.
- The asymmetry between web-shared-foundation (3 files) and its sole consumer web-viewer-application (387 files) creates a high blast-radius risk: any breaking change to data-files.ts or node-culler.ts could affect a large fraction of the viewer application.
- web-shared-foundation is a pure sink in the web package import graph — all 7 cross-zone imports are inbound with zero outbound, making it the only zone whose public API changes carry monorepo-wide ripple risk proportional to the 387-file consumer zone above it.
- web-shared-foundation and mcp-route-layer have no import relationship (direct or transitive through shared types), confirming correct layering: the composition-root tier does not bypass the application tier to access foundation utilities.
- node-culler.ts is documented as having 'zero framework dependencies' with 'Preact integration handled in the CulledNode component' — meaning it is exclusively consumed by viewer UI code. Only data-files.ts is genuinely shared between the server layer (which serves the files) and the viewer layer (which fetches them). Placing an algorithm that is viewer-domain logic in the shared foundation inflates the blast radius of the foundation zone unnecessarily; moving node-culler.ts to src/viewer/shared/ or a viewer-utility sub-module would narrow web-shared-foundation to its true shared-constant purpose.
- The web-shared-foundation zone's public API is consumed by 387 files through a single zone boundary with no versioning or stability contract. data-files.ts exports string constants that are embedded in HTTP fetch URLs — any key rename in DATA_FILES would silently break fetches at runtime since the constant is used on both the server (to serve the file) and the viewer (to request it). This dual-sided consumption makes data-files.ts the highest-blast-radius single point of change in the web package.
- node-culler.ts is a viewer-exclusive IntersectionObserver engine (its docblock explicitly states 'Preact integration is handled in the CulledNode component') but lives in web-shared-foundation, a zone intended to be framework-agnostic and shared between server and viewer. Its presence in the foundation zone inflates web-shared-foundation's blast radius to 387 files for a module that is only ever consumed by the viewer layer. Moving it to src/viewer/ would narrow the foundation to data-files.ts (the only truly shared constant) and reduce unnecessary coupling surface.
- If node-culler.ts is moved to src/viewer/ as recommended, the remaining zone would contain only data-files.ts and index.ts, where index.ts is a pure barrel re-export of data-files.ts. A 2-file zone where one file is solely a barrel of the other provides no grouping abstraction — at that point web-shared-foundation as a zone is functionally equivalent to a single constants module, and dissolving it would reduce zone graph complexity without any architectural downside.
- data-files.ts exports string constants used as HTTP fetch URL keys on both the server (to serve files) and the viewer (to request them). This dual-sided consumption means a key rename in data-files.ts would silently break HTTP fetches at runtime with a 404 — there is no build-time or test-time check that enforces cross-artifact key consistency between the server bundle and the viewer bundle.
- The index.ts barrel in a 3-file (or post-refactor 2-file) zone adds an indirection hop without aggregation value. Barrel files provide value when they consolidate many files for consumers; in a foundation zone this small, having consumers import from data-files.ts directly would reduce indirection without losing any API surface control.
- Add a build-time or test-time validation that the key sets exported from DATA_FILES and SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES in data-files.ts are consistent between the server build artifact and the viewer build artifact. Currently a key rename would silently break HTTP fetches at runtime — this dual-artifact consumption pattern has no compile-time safety net.
- After moving node-culler.ts to src/viewer/, evaluate dissolving web-shared-foundation as a zone: the remaining 2 files (data-files.ts + index.ts barrel) are functionally a single constants module. Inlining data-files.ts into a web-level constants location would eliminate the zone, reduce zone graph complexity, and make the constants' blast radius explicit at the point of definition rather than hidden behind a zone boundary.

</insights>
